zh.Bothal, Northumberland, England: 英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德波塔爾

zh.lifeSummary: Lord Roger Simon De Bertram Of Mitford於大約1225年出生在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德米特福德。他和Baroness Agnes Of Mitford至少有1個兒子。他去世於1273年在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾享年49歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德米特福德。

zh-hans.Mitford, Northumberland, England: 英国英格兰諾圖波藍德米特福德

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lord Roger Simon De Bertram Of Mitford于大约1225年出生在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德米特福德。他和Baroness Agnes Of Mitford至少有1个儿子。他去世于1273年在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾享年49岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德米特福德。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bertram
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German, English (mainly in southeastern Scotland, of Norman origin), French, and Danish: from the ancient Germanic personal name Bertram, composed of the elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. The raven was the bird of Odin, king of the gods, in ancient Germanic mythology. The personal name was common in France throughout the Middle Ages (especially in the form Bertrand ), where its popularity was increased by the fame of the troubadour Bertrand de Born (c. 1140–1214). The name was taken to England by the Normans in the forms Bertran(d), Bertram, and Bartram.

variants.name: Beltrami
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Dante, Dino, Marco, Pio, Sal, Tullio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Beltrame, from an ancient Germanic name composed of the elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. See also Bertram .

variants.name: Bartram
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and North German: variant of   Bertram  .

History: William Bartram, a Quaker, had a large farm near Darby, PA, when his eldest son, John, the first American botanist, was born in 1699. John conducted botanical experiments at his own farm in Kingsessing, PA, near Philadelphia.

variants.name: Bertman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Frieda, Wilhelm.
Americanized form of German Bertmann: altered form (by folk etymology) of Bertram .

variants.name: Bethke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Eldor, Ewald, Otto, Rudi, Wilhelmina.
North German: from a pet form of the ancient Germanic personal names   Bertram   or   Berthold  . See also   Beth  .

variants.name: Bertrand
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Monique, Francois, Gilles, Raoul, Emile, Herve, Jacques, Jean-Marie, Odette.

French, Walloon, and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from the French personal name Bertrand, a variant of Bertram , coined by folk etymology under the influence of the present participle endings -and, -ant.
History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Beltran
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Jesus, Luis, Pedro, Jorge, Mario, Ricardo, Francisco, Rafael.
Spanish (Beltrán), Catalan, and southern French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. See also Bertram .

variants.name: Beltramo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo.
Italian: from the medieval personal name Beltramo, of ancient Germanic origin (see Bertram ).

variants.name: Bert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philibert, Pierre.
German, French, and Polish: from a short form of various compound personal names formed with ancient Germanic berht ‘bright, famous’ (see for example Bertram ).
English: from the Middle English female personal name Berte (Old French Berte, from ancient Germanic Berta ).

variants.name: Battermann
meanings: German:
possibly an occupational name for a merchant or peddler, from Bartermann.
alternatively, perhaps from a short form of the personal name Bertram + Middle High German man ‘man’.

variants.name: Bertran
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Jorge, Eduardo, Enrique, Joaquin, Ricardo, Aleida, Ana, Ana Maria, Elena, Felipe, Jeronimo.  French Andre.
French, Occitan, and Catalan: variant of   Bertram   or   Bertrand  .

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

nameMeanings.name: Simon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

variants.name: Simen
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

en.lifeSummary: Lord Roger Simon De Bertram Of Mitford was born about 1225, in Mitford, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 son with Baroness Agnes Of Mitford. He died in 1273, in Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 49, and was buried in Mitford, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lord Roger Simon De Bertram Of Mitford est né environ 1225, à Mitford, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Baroness Agnes Of Mitford. Il est décédé en 1273, à Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 49 ans, et a été enterré à Mitford, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Lord Roger Simon De Bertram Of Mitford wurde um 1225 in Mitford, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hatte mit Baroness Agnes Of Mitford mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1273 im Alter von 49 Jahren in Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Mitford, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Lord Roger Simon De Bertram Of Mitford è nato il circa 1225 nel Mitford, Northumberland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Baroness Agnes Of Mitford. Morì nel 1273, nel Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 49 anni, e fu sepolto a Mitford, Northumberland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.Bothal, Northumberland, England: イギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドボサル
ja.Mitford, Northumberland, England: イギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドミトフォード
ja.Northamptonshire, England: イギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Lord Roger Simon De Bertram Of Mitfordは約1225年にイギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドミトフォードで生まれた。彼はBaroness Agnes Of Mitfordとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1273年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアで49歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドミトフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lord Roger Simon De Bertram Of Mitford(은)는 대략 1225년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 미트퍼드에서 태어났다. 그는 Baroness Agnes Of Mitford(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1273년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르에서 49세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 미트퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lord Roger Simon De Bertram Of Mitford nasceu aproximadamente 1225, em Mitford, Northumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Baroness Agnes Of Mitford. Ele faleceu em 1273, em Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 49 anos, e foi sepultado em Mitford, Northumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Lord Roger Simon De Bertram Of Mitford родился около 1225 г. в местности митфорд, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Baroness Agnes Of Mitford. Он умер в 1273 г. в местности нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 49 лет и был похоронен в местности митфорд, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Lord Roger Simon De Bertram Of Mitford nació aproximadamente 1225, en Mitford, Northumberland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Baroness Agnes Of Mitford. Murió en 1273, en Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 49 años, y fue sepultado en Mitford, Northumberland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
